title: Comparing and combining conditions
body: Comparisons ask a true/false question: `<` less than, `>` greater than, `==` equal, `!=` not equal, plus `<=` and `>=`. You can *combine* them: `and` needs **both** sides true, `or` needs **at least one**, and `not` flips a true/false. So `age >= 13 and age <= 19` asks "is this a teenager?". The next two challenges practice combining conditions.
